days.label: Day 0 — Friday 3 Apr
days.locationAddress: London Heathrow Airport, Hounslow TW6 1QG, UK

items.activity: LHR Parking — KwikPark Meet & Greet T3
items.notes: ~£119.18 · ⚠️ Book ASAP
items.address: KwikPark Terminal 3, Heathrow Airport, Hounslow TW6 1QG, UK

items.activity: Check in at Heathrow Terminal 3 — VS208
items.notes: Check-in opens ~3 hrs before 13:25 departure
items.address: London Heathrow Airport Terminal 3, Hounslow TW6 1QG, UK

days.label: Day 1 — Saturday 4 Apr
days.locationAddress: Incheon International Airport Terminal 2, 272 Gonghang-daero, Jung-gu, Incheon 22382, South Korea

items.notes: VS208 from LHR. Follow signs to immigration (T2). E-gates available for UK passports. Meet Tourmaker driver in arrivals hall — EVANS FAMILY banner.
items.address: Incheon International Airport Terminal 2, 272 Gonghang-daero, Jung-gu, Incheon 22382, South Korea

items.notes: Allow 60–70 mins through T2 for immigration, baggage reclaim and customs. Driver will be waiting airside exit.
items.address: Crown Park Hotel, 5 Namsandong 2-ga, Jung-gu, Seoul 04605, South Korea

items.activity: Tourmaker taxi — ICN T2 → Crown Park Hotel
items.notes: Ref #1479-1 · 5-seater · Driver WhatsApp eve Thu 3 Apr ~9pm. Journey ~60 mins. Highway toll included.
items.address: Incheon International Airport Terminal 2, 272 Gonghang-daero, Jung-gu, Incheon 22382, South Korea

items.activity: Crown Park Hotel Seoul — drop bags
items.notes: Check-in not until 3pm. Hotel will store luggage. Get changed if needed — youʼve been travelling overnight.
items.address: Crown Park Hotel, 5 Namsandong 2-ga, Jung-gu, Seoul 04605, South Korea

items.notes: SUGGESTION: The hotel is close to Myeongdong. Namdaemun Market is 5 mins walk — one of Seoulʼs oldest markets, great for a first Korean meal. Or stay in Myeongdong for casual street food. · Est. ¥60,000
items.address: Myeongdong Street, Jung-gu, Seoul 04534, South Korea

items.notes: Compact, beautiful royal palace in central Seoul. 20 mins walk from hotel. Watch the Changing of the Guard (2pm most days). Entry ₩1,000pp. Allow 1 hour. · Est. ¥4,000
items.address: Deoksugung Palace, 99 Sejong-daero, Jung-gu, Seoul 04519, South Korea

items.activity: Check in — Crown Park Hotel Seoul
items.notes: Booked via Expedia. Central location near Myeongdong, Namdaemun Market, Namsan Hill.
items.address: Myeongdong Street, Jung-gu, Seoul 04534, South Korea

items.notes: Traditional Korean village with wooden hanok houses. Taxi 15 mins from hotel. Scenic walk, great photos. Best in late afternoon light. Free to explore. · Est. ¥15,000
items.address: Bukchon Hanok Village, Gahoe-dong, Jongno-gu, Seoul 03053, South Korea

items.activity: N Seoul Tower — Namsan cable car
items.notes: SUGGESTION: Cable car ₩9,500pp return · Tower entry ₩21,000pp · Panoramic views over the city. Allow 1.5 hrs. Busy at sunset. · Est. ¥124,000
items.address: N Seoul Tower, 105 Namsangongwon-gil, Yongsan-gu, Seoul 04340, South Korea

items.notes: SUGGESTION: 10 min walk from Namsan. Evening street stalls are excellent — tteokbokki, hotteok, Korean fried chicken. K-beauty shops everywhere. · Est. ¥40,000
items.address: Myeongdong Street, Jung-gu, Seoul 04534, South Korea

items.notes: SUGGESTION: Samgyeopsal (pork belly) or galbi (beef ribs). Many great spots in Myeongdong/Namdaemun area. Estimated ₩60,000–80,000 for 4. · Est. ¥70,000
items.address: Myeongdong Street, Jung-gu, Seoul 04534, South Korea

days.label: Day 2 — Sunday 5 Apr
days.locationAddress: Incheon International Airport Terminal 2, 272 Gonghang-daero, Jung-gu, Incheon 22382, South Korea

items.notes: Last Korean morning. Have a proper sit-down breakfast before the journey.
items.address: Crown Park Hotel, 5 Namsandong 2-ga, Jung-gu, Seoul 04605, South Korea

items.notes: Quick walk to Namdaemun Market if anyone wants last-minute Korean snacks or souvenirs. 5 mins from hotel.
items.address: Namdaemun Market, 21 Namdaemunsijang 4-gil, Jung-gu, Seoul 04532, South Korea

items.activity: Tourmaker taxi — Crown Park → ICN T2
items.notes: Ref #1479-2 · 5-seater · Banner: EVANS FAMILY · Driver WhatsApp eve of Sat 4 Apr ~9pm. Journey ~60 mins to ICN T2.
items.address: Incheon International Airport Terminal 2, 272 Gonghang-daero, Jung-gu, Incheon 22382, South Korea

items.notes: Asiana Airlines counter, Terminal 2. Full luggage check. Allow 30 mins at check-in desk plus 20–30 mins security.
items.address: Incheon International Airport Terminal 2, 272 Gonghang-daero, Jung-gu, Incheon 22382, South Korea

items.notes: Incheon has excellent duty free and a wide range of restaurants airside. Pick up Korean snacks for the flight. · Est. ¥30,000
items.address: Incheon International Airport Terminal 2, 272 Gonghang-daero, Jung-gu, Incheon 22382, South Korea

items.activity: Depart ICN — Asiana OZ108 → Tokyo Narita
items.notes: Flight time approx. 2h 10m. Confirmed booking.
items.address: Incheon International Airport Terminal 2, 272 Gonghang-daero, Jung-gu, Incheon 22382, South Korea

items.notes: Follow signs to JR / bus connections for Shinjuku. Do NOT take taxi — extremely expensive (~¥35,000).
items.address: Shinjuku Station, 3-38-1 Shinjuku, Shinjuku-ku, Tokyo 160-0022, Japan

items.notes: Allow 30–45 mins at immigration. IC card machines are near the exits for purchasing Suica cards (recommended for the whole trip).
items.address: Tokyo Narita International Airport Terminal 1, 1-1 Furugome, Narita, Chiba 282-0004, Japan

items.notes: Pre-booked via Klook app. Highway bus direct to Shinjuku. Comfortable, ~90 mins. Confirm stop and platform at airport.
items.address: Shinjuku Station, 3-38-1 Shinjuku, Shinjuku-ku, Tokyo 160-0022, Japan

items.activity: Check in — Keio Plaza Hotel Tokyo, Shinjuku
items.notes: Base for first 3 nights. West Shinjuku — central for metro, walkable to Kabukicho and Golden Gai.
items.address: Keio Plaza Hotel Tokyo, 2-2-1 Nishishinjuku, Shinjuku-ku, Tokyo 160-8330, Japan

items.notes: A 2-min walk from Keio Plaza. Narrow alleyway of tiny yakitori bars. Cash only, smoky, atmospheric. Perfect first Tokyo dinner. · Est. ¥11,200
items.address: Keio Plaza Hotel Tokyo, 2-2-1 Nishishinjuku, Shinjuku-ku, Tokyo 160-8330, Japan

days.label: Day 3 — Monday 6 Apr
days.locationAddress: Keio Plaza Hotel Tokyo, 2-2-1 Nishishinjuku, Shinjuku-ku, Tokyo 160-8330, Japan

items.notes: Japanese/Western buffet — early start. Fuel up before heading out.
items.address: Keio Plaza Hotel Tokyo, 2-2-1 Nishishinjuku, Shinjuku-ku, Tokyo 160-8330, Japan

items.notes: Direct from hotel to Hamacho (~20 mins). Stable opens 08:00 — arrive a few minutes early.
items.address: Arashio-beya Sumo Stable, 2-47-2 Hama-cho, Nihonbashi, Chuo-ku, Tokyo 103-0007, Japan

items.notes: Watch professional wrestlers (rikishi) train ringside. Intimate and authentic — no barriers, photographers welcome. One of Tokyo's most memorable mornings. Must book in advance via Magical Trip (magicaltripjapan.com) or direct via Arashio-beya website. April cherry blossom peak — fills very fast.
items.address: Arashio-beya Sumo Stable, 2-47-2 Hama-cho, Nihonbashi, Chuo-ku, Tokyo 103-0007, Japan

items.notes: Short hop from Hamacho — 10 mins direct.
items.address: Tsukiji Outer Market, 4-16-2 Tsukiji, Chuo-ku, Tokyo 104-0045, Japan

items.notes: Tamagoyaki, uni, grilled scallops, fresh yakitori. Go stall to stall — most sell out by late morning. Best visited early.
items.address: Tsukiji Outer Market, 4-16-2 Tsukiji, Chuo-ku, Tokyo 104-0045, Japan

items.notes: Direct transfer — no changes needed after morning grazing. Comfortable for the group.
items.address: Asakusa, Taito-ku, Tokyo 111-0032, Japan

items.notes: Oldest Tokyo temple (645 AD). Enter through Kaminarimon Gate, then drop into side streets to beat the crowds. Souvenirs & street snacks.
items.address: Senso-ji Temple, 2-3-1 Asakusa, Taito-ku, Tokyo 111-0032, Japan

items.notes: Local soba, tempura or ramen. Options: Daikokuya (tempura), Namiki Yabusoba (soba).
items.address: Asakusa, Taito-ku, Tokyo 111-0032, Japan

items.notes: Flat, easy 20–25 min walk along the river. Taxi option (~¥900–1,200 total) if feet are tired after the morning.
items.address: Ueno Park, 5-20 Ueno Koen, Taito-ku, Tokyo 110-0007, Japan

items.notes: One of Tokyo's best hanami spots in early April. Wide tree corridors, picnickers, very Japanese atmosphere.
items.address: Ueno Park, 5-20 Ueno Koen, Taito-ku, Tokyo 110-0007, Japan

items.activity: (OPTIONAL) Travel to Imperial Palace East Gardens
items.notes: JR Keihin-Tohoku from Ueno → Tokyo Station (~8 min) + 10 min walk to East Gardens entrance. Drop if time is short.
items.address: Ueno Park, 5-20 Ueno Koen, Taito-ku, Tokyo 110-0007, Japan

items.notes: Tranquil formal gardens — stone walls, ponds, manicured lawns. Peaceful contrast to the urban morning. Skip entirely if time-poor — not essential.
items.address: Imperial Palace East Gardens, 1-1 Chiyoda, Chiyoda-ku, Tokyo 100-8111, Japan

items.notes: JR Chuo Line — Tokyo Station → Shinjuku. Direct, no transfers. Arrive ~4:45pm.
items.address: Shinjuku Station, 3-38-1 Shinjuku, Shinjuku-ku, Tokyo 160-0022, Japan

items.notes: Neon-lit entertainment district on the hotel's doorstep. Huge range of ramen, yakitori and izakaya — walk and pick what looks good.
items.address: Shinjuku Station, 3-38-1 Shinjuku, Shinjuku-ku, Tokyo 160-0022, Japan

items.notes: Six alleys of tiny themed bars — 200 venues, each seating 6–8 people. One drink per bar, local and international crowd. Cash only in most bars.
items.address: Golden Gai, Kabukicho, Shinjuku-ku, Tokyo 160-0021, Japan

days.label: Day 4 — Tuesday 7 Apr
days.locationAddress: Keio Plaza Hotel Tokyo, 2-2-1 Nishishinjuku, Shinjuku-ku, Tokyo 160-8330, Japan

items.notes: Hotel buffet — last relaxed morning before Hakone departure day
items.address: Keio Plaza Hotel Tokyo, 2-2-1 Nishishinjuku, Shinjuku-ku, Tokyo 160-8330, Japan

items.notes: JR Yamanote Line from Shinjuku Station
items.address: Harajuku Station, 1-15-1 Jingumae, Shibuya-ku, Tokyo 150-0001, Japan

items.notes: Major Shinto shrine in forested grounds — serene, ceremonial and a strong cultural contrast to the shopping ahead. Optional ema wishing plaque (¥500–800).
items.address: Meiji Jingu Shrine, 1-1 Yoyogikamizonocho, Shibuya-ku, Tokyo 151-8557, Japan

items.notes: Expansive open park right next door. Locals picnicking, relaxed cherry blossom atmosphere.
items.address: Yoyogi Park, 2-1 Yoyogikamizonocho, Shibuya-ku, Tokyo 151-0052, Japan

items.notes: Takeshita Street (youth fashion energy) · Cat Street (relaxed boutiques) · Chicago (huge thrift/vintage) · Kinji (warehouse-style thrift). Allow 90 mins minimum.
items.address: Harajuku Station, 1-15-1 Jingumae, Shibuya-ku, Tokyo 150-0001, Japan

items.notes: Tree-lined Parisian-style boulevard, refined cafés and stylish restaurants. Walk ~10 mins south from Harajuku. Options: Afuri (yuzu ramen), Maisen (tonkatsu).
items.address: Harajuku Station, 1-15-1 Jingumae, Shibuya-ku, Tokyo 150-0001, Japan

items.notes: Pleasant 15–20 min flat walk from Omotesando. Do not take metro — the walk is the experience.
items.address: Shibuya Crossing, 2-2-1 Dogenzaka, Shibuya-ku, Tokyo 150-0043, Japan

items.notes: Shibuya 109 (youth fashion) · Loft & Hands (stationery/design) · Tower Records (music). Tokyo at its most urban.
items.address: Shibuya Crossing, 2-2-1 Dogenzaka, Shibuya-ku, Tokyo 150-0043, Japan

items.notes: Sit and recharge. Best options: % Arabica · Blue Bottle · Streamer Coffee. All within 5 mins of Shibuya crossing.
items.address: Shibuya Crossing, 2-2-1 Dogenzaka, Shibuya-ku, Tokyo 150-0043, Japan

items.notes: Record stores, vintage sneakers, manga shops, boutiques, graphic tees. Let Jack and Charlie lead — this is the best part.
items.address: Shibuya Crossing, 2-2-1 Dogenzaka, Shibuya-ku, Tokyo 150-0043, Japan

items.notes: Ramen, izakaya or yakiniku. Options: Uobei (conveyor belt sushi), Han no Daidokoro (yakiniku). Book ahead for busy evenings.
items.address: Shibuya Crossing, 2-2-1 Dogenzaka, Shibuya-ku, Tokyo 150-0043, Japan

items.notes: JR Yamanote Line Shibuya → Shinjuku. Quick and direct.
items.address: Shibuya Crossing, 2-2-1 Dogenzaka, Shibuya-ku, Tokyo 150-0043, Japan

days.label: Day 5 — Wednesday 8 Apr
days.locationAddress: Shinjuku Station, 3-38-1 Shinjuku, Shinjuku-ku, Tokyo 160-0022, Japan

items.notes: Last Tokyo morning – hotel buffet
items.address: Keio Plaza Hotel Tokyo, 2-2-1 Nishishinjuku, Shinjuku-ku, Tokyo 160-8330, Japan

items.notes: Leave bags at hotel reception until departure
items.address: Keio Plaza Hotel Tokyo, 2-2-1 Nishishinjuku, Shinjuku-ku, Tokyo 160-8330, Japan

items.notes: Hotel moving walkway → West Exit / Odakyu Line (750m)
items.address: Shinjuku Station, 3-38-1 Shinjuku, Shinjuku-ku, Tokyo 160-0022, Japan

items.notes: Odakyu Sightseeing Centre, West Exit — ¥8,000/person (3-day adult, covers all Hakone transport + Shinjuku↔Odawara for 8–10 Apr inclusive)
items.address: Shinjuku Station, 3-38-1 Shinjuku, Shinjuku-ku, Tokyo 160-0022, Japan

items.notes: Car 1, rows 5–8, D seats (right window) for Mt. Fuji views. Surcharge only — base fare covered by Freepass. Book EMot/odakyu.jp on Mon 9 March.
items.address: Shinjuku Station, 3-38-1 Shinjuku, Shinjuku-ku, Tokyo 160-0022, Japan

items.notes: Transfer to Tozan mountain railway
items.address: Hakone-Yumoto Station, 707 Yumoto, Hakone, Ashigarashimo-gun, Kanagawa 250-0311, Japan

items.notes: Rack railway climbs through cedar forest, waterfalls visible from windows
items.address: Keio Plaza Hotel Tokyo, 2-2-1 Nishishinjuku, Shinjuku-ku, Tokyo 160-8330, Japan
Keio Plaza Hotel Tokyo, 2-2-1 Nishishinjuku, Shinjuku-ku, Tokyo 160-8330, Japan

items.notes: Every 15–20 min from Gora Station Stop #1. No booking needed
items.address: TEN-YU Ryokan, 1297 Sengokuhara, Hakone, Ashigarashimo-gun, Kanagawa 250-0631, Japan

items.notes: Reception holds all bags until 3pm check-in. Take day bag only for museum visit
items.address: TEN-YU Ryokan, 1297 Sengokuhara, Hakone, Ashigarashimo-gun, Kanagawa 250-0631, Japan

items.notes: Return for Open Air Museum detour
items.address: Keio Plaza Hotel Tokyo, 2-2-1 Nishishinjuku, Shinjuku-ku, Tokyo 160-8330, Japan

items.notes: One stop back down the mountain
items.address: Keio Plaza Hotel Tokyo, 2-2-1 Nishishinjuku, Shinjuku-ku, Tokyo 160-8330, Japan

items.notes: 120+ sculptures incl. Rodin, Moore, Picasso. Outdoor foot onsen at end. Allow 2 hrs
items.address: Hakone Open Air Museum, 1121 Ninotaira, Hakone, Kanagawa 250-0492, Japan

items.notes: Return up the mountain
items.address: Keio Plaza Hotel Tokyo, 2-2-1 Nishishinjuku, Shinjuku-ku, Tokyo 160-8330, Japan

items.notes: 3pm check-in. Superior Rooms with private open-air rotenburo bath
items.address: TEN-YU Ryokan, 1297 Sengokuhara, Hakone, Ashigarashimo-gun, Kanagawa 250-0631, Japan

items.notes: ¥5,605 F&B credit per room included. Buffet style in hotel restaurant
items.address: Keio Plaza Hotel Tokyo, 2-2-1 Nishishinjuku, Shinjuku-ku, Tokyo 160-8330, Japan

items.notes: In-room outdoor hot spring bath. Best experienced after dark
items.address: Keio Plaza Hotel Tokyo, 2-2-1 Nishishinjuku, Shinjuku-ku, Tokyo 160-8330, Japan

days.label: Day 6 — Thursday 9 Apr
days.locationAddress: TEN-YU Ryokan, 1297 Sengokuhara, Hakone, Ashigarashimo-gun, Kanagawa 250-0631, Japan

items.notes: Full kaiseki/Western buffet at the hotel. Take your time — no early start needed today.
items.address: TEN-YU Ryokan, 1297 Sengokuhara, Hakone, Ashigarashimo-gun, Kanagawa 250-0631, Japan

items.notes: Free shuttle every 15–20 mins. Day 2 of 3-day Freepass.
items.address: TEN-YU Ryokan, 1297 Sengokuhara, Hakone, Ashigarashimo-gun, Kanagawa 250-0631, Japan

items.notes: Hakone Tozan Cable Car climbs steeply through dense cedar forest. 4 stations to the top.
items.address: TEN-YU Ryokan, 1297 Sengokuhara, Hakone, Ashigarashimo-gun, Kanagawa 250-0631, Japan

items.notes: Aerial gondola over volcanic ridgeline. On clear mornings, Mt. Fuji perfectly framed ahead.
items.address: Hakone Ropeway Sounzan Station, Gora, Hakone, Kanagawa 250-0408, Japan

items.notes: Active volcanic zone — sulphur vents, lunar landscape. Black eggs (kurotamago, ¥600 per 5-egg bag) cooked in volcanic hot springs. Buy a bag. Note: ropeway occasionally closes due to volcanic activity — check on the day.
items.address: Hakone Ropeway Sounzan Station, Gora, Hakone, Kanagawa 250-0408, Japan

items.notes: Final descent to Lake Ashi through volcanic terrain.
items.address: Hakone Ropeway Sounzan Station, Gora, Hakone, Kanagawa 250-0408, Japan

items.activity: Lake Ashi — Hakone Pirate Ship Cruise
items.notes: Iconic sightseeing cruise across the caldera lake. On clear days, Fuji perfectly reflected in the water. Disembark at Moto-Hakone.
items.address: Togendai Pier, 164 Motohakone, Hakone, Kanagawa 250-0522, Japan

items.notes: Tranquil lakeside village. Hakone Shrine is a 5-min walk from the pier.
items.address: Hakone Shrine, 80-1 Motohakone, Hakone, Ashigarashimo-gun, Kanagawa 250-0522, Japan

items.notes: Ancient cedar path leads to the famous red torii gate rising from the lake — one of Japan's most photographed spots. Free to enter.
items.address: Hakone Shrine, 80-1 Motohakone, Hakone, Ashigarashimo-gun, Kanagawa 250-0522, Japan

items.notes: Relaxed setting with lake views. Yuba (silky tofu skin) is a local Hakone speciality. Several options within 5 min of the shrine.
items.address: TEN-YU Ryokan, 1297 Sengokuhara, Hakone, Ashigarashimo-gun, Kanagawa 250-0631, Japan

items.notes: Izu Hakone or Hakone Tozan bus — all covered by Freepass. Direct or change at Hakone-machi.
items.address: Hakone-Yumoto Station, 707 Yumoto, Hakone, Ashigarashimo-gun, Kanagawa 250-0311, Japan

items.notes: Soak in the private open-air bath as the light fades. Best enjoyed before dinner.
items.address: TEN-YU Ryokan, 1297 Sengokuhara, Hakone, Ashigarashimo-gun, Kanagawa 250-0631, Japan

items.notes: Multi-course buffet with seasonal Japanese dishes. Take your time — included in room rate. Final night at the ryokan.
items.address: TEN-YU Ryokan, 1297 Sengokuhara, Hakone, Ashigarashimo-gun, Kanagawa 250-0631, Japan

days.label: Day 7 — Friday 10 Apr
days.locationAddress: TEN-YU Ryokan, 1297 Sengokuhara, Hakone, Ashigarashimo-gun, Kanagawa 250-0631, Japan

items.notes: Last morning at the ryokan. Savour the buffet — check-out is 11:00am.
items.address: TEN-YU Ryokan, 1297 Sengokuhara, Hakone, Ashigarashimo-gun, Kanagawa 250-0631, Japan

items.notes: Last soak in the private open-air bath. Pack and bring luggage to reception.
items.address: TEN-YU Ryokan, 1297 Sengokuhara, Hakone, Ashigarashimo-gun, Kanagawa 250-0631, Japan

items.notes: Luggage collected at reception. 3-day Freepass still active — Day 3.
items.address: TEN-YU Ryokan, 1297 Sengokuhara, Hakone, Ashigarashimo-gun, Kanagawa 250-0631, Japan

items.notes: Free shuttle from TEN-YU front — last ride.
items.address: TEN-YU Ryokan, 1297 Sengokuhara, Hakone, Ashigarashimo-gun, Kanagawa 250-0631, Japan

items.notes: Mountain railway descent through cedar forest. Scenic even on the way down.
items.address: Hakone-Yumoto Station, 707 Yumoto, Hakone, Ashigarashimo-gun, Kanagawa 250-0311, Japan

items.notes: Last journey on the 3-day Hakone Freepass. Transfer to JR Shinkansen platforms at Odawara. — Book via Klook (SmartEX not available in UK). Green Car D seats rows 10–15.
items.address: Hakone-Yumoto Station, 707 Yumoto, Hakone, Ashigarashimo-gun, Kanagawa 250-0311, Japan

items.notes: JR Shinkansen platforms upstairs. Buy station bento boxes — Odawara is famous for kamaboko (fish cakes). Eat on the train. — Book via Klook (SmartEX not available in UK). Green Car D seats rows 10–15.
items.address: Odawara Station, 1-1-1 Jonai-cho, Odawara, Kanagawa 250-0011, Japan

items.activity: Hikari Shinkansen: Odawara → Kyoto — Book via Klook (SmartEX not available in UK). Green Car D seats rows 10–15.
items.notes: Green Car, D seats rows 10–15 (right window). Mt. Fuji visible on right between Odawara and Mishima — first ~30 mins. Book via Klook from Tue 10 March. Confirm exact departure time when booking.
items.address: Odawara Station, 1-1-1 Jonai-cho, Odawara, Kanagawa 250-0011, Japan

items.notes: One of Japan's most dramatic station buildings — worth a few mins to look around.
items.address: Kyoto Station, Karasuma-dori, Shimogyo-ku, Kyoto 600-8216, Japan

items.notes: Karasuma subway line northbound — 3 stops, 6 mins. Exit Shijo, 5 min walk east to hotel.
items.address: Kyoto Station, Karasuma-dori, Shimogyo-ku, Kyoto 600-8216, Japan

items.activity: Check in — Mitsui Garden Hotel Kyoto
items.notes: Central Kyoto location — walking distance to Gion, Nishiki Market and Ponto-cho. Base for 4 nights.
items.address: Mitsui Garden Hotel Kyoto Shijo, 669 Banzai-cho, Shimogyo-ku, Kyoto 600-8096, Japan

items.notes: Atmospheric riverside alley dining. In cherry blossom season, some restaurants open kawayuka (outdoor terraces over the Kamo River). Walk and look in — no reservation needed.
items.address: Gion District, Hanamikoji-dori, Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto 605-0074, Japan

days.label: Day 8 — Saturday 11 Apr
days.locationAddress: Mitsui Garden Hotel Kyoto Shijo, 669 Banzai-cho, Shimogyo-ku, Kyoto 600-8096, Japan

items.notes: Hotel breakfast. First full Kyoto day.
items.address: Mitsui Garden Hotel Kyoto Shijo, 669 Banzai-cho, Shimogyo-ku, Kyoto 600-8096, Japan

items.notes: Karasuma Line northbound to Kitaoji Station (¥260pp). 15 min ride. Day 1 of northern temple loop. · Est. ¥1,040
items.address: Mitsui Garden Hotel Kyoto Shijo, 669 Banzai-cho, Shimogyo-ku, Kyoto 600-8096, Japan

items.notes: Iconic zen garden temple; gold-leaf pavilion reflecting in Mirror Pond. One of Kyotoʼs most visited sites — arrive early. ¥1,200pp. Allow 45–60 min. · Est. ¥4,800
items.address: Kinkaku-ji Temple, 1 Kinkakujicho, Kita-ku, Kyoto 603-8361, Japan

items.notes: Famous karesansui (dry rock garden) — 15 stones, no two ever visible at once. Zen and meditative. ¥800pp. Allow 45 min. · Est. ¥3,200
items.address: Ryoan-ji Temple, 13 Ryoanjigoranocho, Ukyo-ku, Kyoto 616-8001, Japan

items.notes: Bus from Ryoan-ji to Ginkaku-ji (covered by Kyoto bus day pass ¥700/person — buy at bus terminals). Or taxi ~¥1,200. · Est. ¥2,800
items.address: Ryoan-ji Temple, 13 Ryoanjigoranocho, Ukyo-ku, Kyoto 616-8001, Japan

items.notes: Eastern foothills of Kyoto. Famous Zen garden and "Sea of Silver Sand" moss garden. ¥1,100pp. Quieter than Kinkaku-ji. · Est. ¥4,400
items.address: Kinkaku-ji Temple, 1 Kinkakujicho, Kita-ku, Kyoto 603-8361, Japan

items.notes: 2km cherry tree-lined canal path from Ginkaku-ji towards Nanzen-ji. One of Kyoto's most beautiful walks in cherry blossom season. Cafés and craft shops along the way.
items.address: Ginkaku-ji Temple, 2 Ginkakujicho, Sakyo-ku, Kyoto 606-8402, Japan

items.notes: Several excellent lunch spots near Nanzen-ji: yudofu (tofu hotpot, a Kyoto speciality), or Omen noodle restaurant on the Path. · Est. ¥10,000
items.address: Nanzen-ji Temple, 86 Fukuchicho, Sakyo-ku, Kyoto 606-8435, Japan

items.activity: Return to hotel — rest before Gion
items.notes: Subway from Keage or Higashiyama Station back to Shijo. ¥220pp. · Est. ¥880
items.address: Gion District, Hanamikoji-dori, Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto 605-0074, Japan

items.notes: Book ahead: try Tempura Endo (counter tempura) or a traditional kaiseki set if budget allows. Or walk Hanamikoji and find an izakaya. Gion fills fast on Saturdays. · Est. ¥16,000
items.address: Gion District, Hanamikoji-dori, Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto 605-0074, Japan

items.notes: Gionʼs most atmospheric street — beautifully lit machiya townhouses. Walk to Yasaka Shrine at the top (free, open all night, stunning lanterns).
items.address: Gion District, Hanamikoji-dori, Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto 605-0074, Japan

days.label: Day 9 — Sunday 12 Apr
days.locationAddress: Mitsui Garden Hotel Kyoto Shijo, 669 Banzai-cho, Shimogyo-ku, Kyoto 600-8096, Japan

items.notes: Hotel breakfast. Pack light day bag — full day outside.
items.address: Mitsui Garden Hotel Kyoto Shijo, 669 Banzai-cho, Shimogyo-ku, Kyoto 600-8096, Japan

items.notes: Several rental shops around hotel or near Arashiyama Station. E-bikes ~¥1,500–2,000pp per day. Book ahead on weekends in cherry blossom season. · Est. ¥8,000
items.address: Togetsukyo Bridge, Sagatenryuji Susukinobabacho, Ukyo-ku, Kyoto 616-0007, Japan

items.notes: Pleasant 35–40 min cycle west along the Kamo River. Arashiyama is a village at the edge of the mountains. Togetsukyo (Moon Crossing Bridge) is the landmark arrival point.
items.address: Togetsukyo Bridge, Sagatenryuji Susukinobabacho, Ukyo-ku, Kyoto 616-0007, Japan

items.notes: Walk the bamboo path — most dramatic in the morning light. Go early — it fills by midday. Free to walk through. 10 min from the bridge.
items.address: Arashiyama Bamboo Grove, Sagaogurayama, Ukyo-ku, Kyoto 616-0006, Japan

items.notes: UNESCO World Heritage Rinzai Zen temple with a stunning garden designed 700 years ago. ¥1,200pp for garden only (¥500 extra for main hall). Allow 90 min. · Est. ¥4,800
items.address: Tenryu-ji Temple, 68 Sagatenryujisunomicho, Ukyo-ku, Kyoto 616-8385, Japan

items.notes: Hike 20 min up the mountain above Arashiyama to find 120+ wild Japanese macaques. ¥1,300pp. Great views over the valley. Kids will love it. · Est. ¥5,200
items.address: Togetsukyo Bridge, Sagatenryuji Susukinobabacho, Ukyo-ku, Kyoto 616-0007, Japan

items.notes: Excellent options along the waterfront: tofu cuisine, soba, kaiseki lunch sets. The riverside setting is beautiful in cherry blossom season. · Est. ¥14,000
items.address: Togetsukyo Bridge, Sagatenryuji Susukinobabacho, Ukyo-ku, Kyoto 616-0007, Japan

items.notes: Cycle north into the quieter Sagano area — rice fields, small shrines, very local atmosphere. Jack and Charlie can lead.
items.address: Arashiyama Bamboo Grove, Sagaogurayama, Ukyo-ku, Kyoto 616-0006, Japan

items.activity: Return bikes — cycle back to Kyoto
items.notes: Return via Katsura River cycling path. Scenic ~40 min ride back to central Kyoto.
items.address: Mitsui Garden Hotel Kyoto Shijo, 669 Banzai-cho, Shimogyo-ku, Kyoto 600-8096, Japan

items.notes: Kyotoʼs most atmospheric dining alley. In April, many restaurants open their kawayuka (outdoor terraces over the Kamo River). Walk and peer in — book ahead for riverside seats. · Est. ¥16,000
items.address: Pontocho, Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto 604-8094, Japan

days.label: Day 10 — Monday 13 Apr
days.locationAddress: Mitsui Garden Hotel Kyoto Shijo, 669 Banzai-cho, Shimogyo-ku, Kyoto 600-8096, Japan

items.notes: Hotel breakfast before early departure.
items.address: Mitsui Garden Hotel Kyoto Shijo, 669 Banzai-cho, Shimogyo-ku, Kyoto 600-8096, Japan

items.notes: JR Pass covered. Direct, 45 min. Depart from Kyoto Station (short walk or subway from hotel). IC cards work for subway to Kyoto Station. · Est. ¥880
items.address: Kyoto Station, Karasuma-dori, Shimogyo-ku, Kyoto 600-8216, Japan

items.notes: Walk east from Nara Station (~10 min). Over 1,200 free-roaming sika deer who will bow for crackers (shika senbei ¥200/pack). Buy several packs. · Est. ¥1,600
items.address: Nara Park, 469 Zoshicho, Nara 630-8211, Japan

items.notes: Largest wooden structure in the world. Houses a 15m bronze Buddha. ¥1,400pp. Allow 90 min — impressive approach through Nandaimon gate. Very popular but huge space. · Est. ¥5,600
items.address: Todai-ji Temple, 406-1 Zoshicho, Nara 630-8211, Japan

items.notes: Ancient Shinto shrine deep in the forest. Hundreds of stone and bronze lanterns lining the path. Free to walk the approach. Serene contrast to Todai-ji.
items.address: Todai-ji Temple, 406-1 Zoshicho, Nara 630-8211, Japan

items.notes: Walk 15 min south into the old merchant quarter. Good kakinoha-zushi (persimmon leaf sushi, Naraʼs speciality) and mochi shops. Quieter than the park area. · Est. ¥7,200
items.address: Nara Park, 469 Zoshicho, Nara 630-8211, Japan

items.notes: Preserved Edo-era merchant houses turned into craft shops, sake merchants and galleries. Unhurried and very local. Good souvenir hunting.
items.address: Nara Park, 469 Zoshicho, Nara 630-8211, Japan

items.notes: JR Pass covered. Final evening in Kyoto — pack tonight ready for tomorrowʼs hotel move. · Est. ¥880
items.address: Mitsui Garden Hotel Kyoto Shijo, 669 Banzai-cho, Shimogyo-ku, Kyoto 600-8096, Japan

items.activity: Final Kyoto evening — Nishiki Market browse
items.notes: Kyotoʼs covered food market (last stalls close ~18:00). Last chance for Kyoto pickles, yuba tofu and matcha sweets to take home.
items.address: Nishiki Market, 609 Tominokujicho, Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto 604-8054, Japan

items.notes: Last night in Kyoto. Splurge a little — traditional kaiseki set menu or high-quality kaiseki donburi. Many excellent options in the Shijo/Gion area. · Est. ¥16,000
items.address: Gion District, Hanamikoji-dori, Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto 605-0074, Japan

days.label: Day 11 — Tuesday 14 Apr
days.locationAddress: Mitsui Garden Hotel Kyoto Shijo, 669 Banzai-cho, Shimogyo-ku, Kyoto 600-8096, Japan

items.notes: Hotel breakfast. Last Kyoto morning. Pack all luggage.
items.address: Mitsui Garden Hotel Kyoto Shijo, 669 Banzai-cho, Shimogyo-ku, Kyoto 600-8096, Japan

items.notes: SUGGESTION: 90 min tea ceremony experience near Fushimi Inari (~15 min taxi). Multiple operators in Kyoto. Book in advance. Peaceful way to spend the last Kyoto morning. · Est. ¥16,000
items.address: Camellia Tea Experience, 184-6 Shimogawaracho, Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto 605-0814, Japan

items.notes: Grand vermilion torii gate — 24m tall, one of Japanʼs largest. Free to approach. Garden entry ¥1,500pp but optional. 5 min walk from Okazaki area. · Est. ¥6,000
items.address: Heian Jingu Shrine, 97 Nishi Tennocho, Okazaki, Sakyo-ku, Kyoto 606-8341, Japan

items.activity: Check out — Mitsui Garden Hotel Kyoto
items.notes: 4-night stay. Confirm luggage is ready. Short walk or subway to Kyoto Station.
items.address: Kyoto Station, Karasuma-dori, Shimogyo-ku, Kyoto 600-8216, Japan

items.activity: JR Shinkansen / Special Rapid: Kyoto → Osaka
items.notes: JR Pass covered. Fastest: Shinkansen (15 min) or JR Special Rapid (30 min, no surcharge). Osaka Station is central.
items.address: Odawara Station, 1-1-1 Jonai-cho, Odawara, Kanagawa 250-0011, Japan

items.activity: Check in — Candeo Hotels Osaka The Tower
items.notes: Or drop bags if too early. Booking confirmed. Excellent location — 10 min walk to Dotonbori.
items.address: Candeo Hotels Osaka The Tower, 5-2-28 Shibata, Kita-ku, Osaka 530-0012, Japan

items.notes: Walk the famous canal strip. Glico Running Man photo, Kani Doraku crab sign. Vibrant, loud and very Osaka. Let everyone explore at their own pace.
items.address: Dotonbori, Chuo-ku, Osaka 542-0071, Japan

items.notes: Osaka's answer to Harajuku. Vintage stores, streetwear, record shops. 15 min walk from Dotonbori. Jack and Charlie will want time here.
items.address: Harajuku Station, 1-15-1 Jingumae, Shibuya-ku, Tokyo 150-0001, Japan

items.notes: Osaka is Japanʼs food capital. Options: Ichiran Ramen (solo booths), takoyaki crawl, Kani Doraku (crab, reserve), or izakaya with kushikatsu. Budget ~¥3,000pp. · Est. ¥12,000
items.address: Dotonbori, Chuo-ku, Osaka 542-0071, Japan

days.label: Day 12 — Wednesday 15 Apr
days.locationAddress: Candeo Hotels Osaka The Tower, 5-2-28 Shibata, Kita-ku, Osaka 530-0012, Japan

items.notes: Hotel breakfast or grab from a nearby konbini (7-Eleven level of quality in Japan).
items.address: Candeo Hotels Osaka The Tower, 5-2-28 Shibata, Kita-ku, Osaka 530-0012, Japan

items.notes: Subway or walk ~30 min. Tanimachi 4-chome Station (Tanimachi Line) is closest. · Est. ¥840
items.address: Osaka Castle, 1-1 Osakajo, Chuo-ku, Osaka 540-0002, Japan

items.notes: One of Japanʼs most famous castles. ¥1,400pp for 8F observation deck. Museum inside. The surrounding park (free) is excellent for a walk — cherry blossoms in early-mid April. · Est. ¥5,600
items.address: Osaka Castle, 1-1 Osakajo, Chuo-ku, Osaka 540-0002, Japan

items.notes: Large park around the moat — very pleasant, lots of locals. Free. Allow 45 min circular walk.
items.address: Osaka Castle, 1-1 Osakajo, Chuo-ku, Osaka 540-0002, Japan

items.notes: 170 stalls of fresh seafood, wagyu, fruit and street food. Eat as you walk — uni, fresh oysters, grilled wagyu skewers. Can be expensive but worth grazing. Called ʼOsakaʼs kitchenʼ. · Est. ¥12,000
items.address: Kuromon Ichiba Market, 2-4-1 Nipponbashi, Naniwa-ku, Osaka 556-0005, Japan

items.notes: 1920s-era entertainment district — retro neon signs, cheap kushikatsu (breadcrumbed skewers) restaurants. Very local and fun contrast to modern Osaka. Try the Tsutenkaku Tower area.
items.address: Shinsekai, Naniwa-ku, Osaka 556-0002, Japan

items.notes: Osaka's answer to Tokyo Tower — kitsch and beloved. Observation deck ¥2,000pp. Blowfish (fugu) motifs everywhere (itʼs a delicacy in Shinsekai). Allow 1 hour. · Est. ¥8,000
items.address: Shinsekai, Naniwa-ku, Osaka 556-0002, Japan

items.notes: Osaka's Akihabara. Retro game shops, Gunpla (Gundam models), anime figures. Jack and Charlie in their element.
items.address: Den Den Town, Nipponbashi, Naniwa-ku, Osaka 556-0005, Japan

items.notes: Covered arcade shopping street — longest in Japan. Good for last souvenirs, Japanese beauty products, and fashion.
items.address: Shinsaibashi, Chuo-ku, Osaka 542-0085, Japan

items.notes: Splurge option: proper yakiniku (BBQ your own wagyu at the table). Budget ~¥4,000–5,000pp. Excellent options in Namba area. Book ahead. · Est. ¥18,000
items.address: Namba, Chuo-ku, Osaka 542-0075, Japan

days.label: Day 13 — Thursday 16 Apr
days.locationAddress: Candeo Hotels Osaka The Tower, 5-2-28 Shibata, Kita-ku, Osaka 530-0012, Japan

items.notes: Early-ish start for a big day. Hotel breakfast — fuel up well.
items.address: Candeo Hotels Osaka The Tower, 5-2-28 Shibata, Kita-ku, Osaka 530-0012, Japan

items.notes: Candeo Hotels is a short walk to Osaka Station (JR). Head to the JR Special Rapid platforms.
items.address: Osaka Station, 3-1-1 Umeda, Kita-ku, Osaka 530-0001, Japan

items.activity: JR Special Rapid: Osaka → Sannomiya, Kobe
items.notes: JR Pass fully covered. Approx 25 min direct. Alight at Sannomiya Station — the main Kobe hub. Not Shin-Kobe (Shinkansen station).
items.address: Sannomiya Station, Chuo-ku, Kobe, Hyogo 651-0095, Japan

items.activity: Arrive Kobe Sannomiya — walk to Kitano-cho
items.notes: Walk north uphill from Sannomiya Station along Kitanozaka hill — about 15 mins on foot. The climb is gentle and the streets get more atmospheric as you go. Pick up a map at the tourist info booth at the station exit.
items.address: Kitano-cho, Chuo-ku, Kobe, Hyogo 650-0002, Japan

items.notes: Historic foreigner settlement district from the Meiji era — about 20 preserved Western mansions (Ijinkan) built by foreign merchants and diplomats after Kobe port opened in 1868. The whole area is atmospheric and very walkable. Start at Kitano-cho Square — bronze statue of a musician with the iconic Kazamidori (Weathercock House) behind it: great photo stop. Built in 1904 by a German trader, Kazamidori is the symbol of the district. Next: Uroko House (Fish Scale House) — named for its overlapping slate siding resembling fish scales; small European art museum inside. If the kids want something odd, Ben's House has full taxidermy including a tiger and polar bear. The Dutch House has a perfume workshop where you can make your own scent (great for the group). Combination tickets available: Yamanote Discount Pass (4 houses + observation gallery) ¥2,200pp adults. The streets between houses have good cafés — stop for coffee here. Allow ~90 mins total including walking.
items.address: Kitano-cho, Chuo-ku, Kobe, Hyogo 650-0002, Japan

items.activity: Walk back down to Sannomiya Station
items.notes: 15 min walk downhill. If anyone wants a quick Kobe beef bite before departing, Steakland Kobe on the way back to the station is affordable and fast — Kobe beef set lunch from ~¥3,500pp. Skip if time is tight.
items.address: Sannomiya Station, Chuo-ku, Kobe, Hyogo 651-0095, Japan

items.notes: JR Pass covered. Direct train, approx 40 min. Sit on the right side for views of the Harima Plain. Himeji Castle is visible from the train as you approach the city.
items.address: Himeji Station, 68 Ekimae-cho, Himeji, Hyogo 670-0927, Japan

items.activity: Arrive Himeji Station — walk to castle
items.notes: The castle is visible straight ahead from the north exit — a perfectly straight 15 min walk up Otemon-dori boulevard. You can literally see it from the moment you step outside. Look up and walk towards it.
items.address: Himeji Castle, 68 Honmachi, Himeji, Hyogo 670-0012, Japan

items.notes: Several good restaurants along the approach boulevard between the station and the castle. Himeji specialities: anago (conger eel) over rice — try Maneken or Fukutei on Otemon-dori. Also good for a quick set lunch before the castle. Budget ¥1,500–2,500pp. · Est. ¥8,000
items.address: Himeji Castle, 68 Honmachi, Himeji, Hyogo 670-0012, Japan

items.notes: Buy the combined castle + Koko-en Garden combo ticket at the main ticket gate: ¥2,600pp adults (price increased March 2026), under 18 free. The combo ticket lets you skip the queue at Koko-en Garden later — worth it. English audio guide available for ¥200 — recommended.
items.address: Himeji Castle, 68 Honmachi, Himeji, Hyogo 670-0012, Japan

items.notes: UNESCO World Heritage and Japan's finest surviving feudal castle — never destroyed, never rebuilt. The original 17th-century interior is completely intact. The approach path winds and doubles back deliberately — it was designed to confuse attackers, and you walk that same defensive route. Look out for: stone-dropping windows (ishiotoshi) at the base of walls for dropping rocks on invaders; loopholes (sama) shaped as circles, triangles and rectangles for arrows and guns; hidden rooms built into walls for ambush positions. The 7-storey keep has over 100 steep wooden stairs — no elevator, wear comfortable shoes. Views from the top floor over the city and the Harima Plain are excellent. Approx 1,000 cherry trees line the moats and paths — mid-April may be slightly past peak but expect blossom or late blooms on the castle grounds. Allow 90 min minimum. · Est. ¥10,400 (combo tickets × 4 adults, under-18s free)
items.address: Himeji Castle, 68 Honmachi, Himeji, Hyogo 670-0012, Japan

items.notes: Jack leaves the castle at ~14:00, walks back to Himeji Station (15 min). JR Special Rapid: Himeji → Osaka direct, approx 60 min. Arrives Osaka ~15:05 — in time for the 15:00 commitment. JR Pass covers the journey. Train runs every 15–20 mins from Himeji Station platform 2.
items.address: Himeji Station, 68 Ekimae-cho, Himeji, Hyogo 670-0927, Japan

items.notes: Exit the castle and cross the moat bridge to Koko-en — about 5 min walk west of the main castle gate. Nine separate walled Edo-period gardens on the former site of the castle lord's west residence. Each garden has a different theme: the largest (Oyashiki no Niwa) has a huge koi pond with 200+ Nishiki koi; the Tea Ceremony Garden has a teahouse where you can sit and order matcha and sweets (recommended — allow 20 mins). Also: bamboo garden, pine garden, flower garden. The castle provides a dramatic backdrop throughout. Combo ticket bypasses the entry queue. Allow 45–60 min. Especially beautiful in cherry blossom season.
items.address: Koko-en Garden, 68 Honmachi, Himeji, Hyogo 670-0012, Japan

items.notes: Covered arcade street running between the castle and Himeji Station — you'll walk through it on the way back. Good for Himeji-specific omiyage (gifts): tama tsubaki (mochi sweets with yellow azuki bean paste), local sake, leather goods (Himeji is known for traditional leather craft). Also karintou (black sugar crackers) if you want something for the flight home.
items.address: Himeji Station, 68 Ekimae-cho, Himeji, Hyogo 670-0927, Japan

items.notes: JR Pass covered. Direct, approx 60 min. Arrive Osaka ~17:20.
items.address: Candeo Hotels Osaka The Tower, 5-2-28 Shibata, Kita-ku, Osaka 530-0012, Japan

items.activity: Back at Candeo — rest & freshen up
items.notes: Long but excellent day. Drop bags, feet up, recharge before final dinner.
items.address: Candeo Hotels Osaka The Tower, 5-2-28 Shibata, Kita-ku, Osaka 530-0012, Japan

items.activity: Final Japan dinner — best of Osaka
items.notes: Last full evening in Japan — make it count. Osaka is Japan's food capital. Options: high-end unagi (eel) at Ichiryu, top ramen at Kinryu Ramen on Dotonbori, or revisit a favourite from earlier in the trip. Budget ¥4,000–5,000pp. · Est. ¥18,000
items.address: Dotonbori, Chuo-ku, Osaka 542-0071, Japan

days.label: Day 14 — Friday 17 Apr
days.locationAddress: Candeo Hotels Osaka The Tower, 5-2-28 Shibata, Kita-ku, Osaka 530-0012, Japan

items.notes: Final Japan breakfast.
items.address: Candeo Hotels Osaka The Tower, 5-2-28 Shibata, Kita-ku, Osaka 530-0012, Japan

items.notes: Last chance! Konbini snack haul, KitKat variety packs, Japanese stationery, sunscreen. Namba Don Quijote opens at 09:00.
items.address: Namba, Chuo-ku, Osaka 542-0075, Japan

items.notes: Store luggage at hotel if needed for a couple of hours. Checkout 11:00am.
items.address: Candeo Hotels Osaka The Tower, 5-2-28 Shibata, Kita-ku, Osaka 530-0012, Japan

items.notes: Options: JR Haruka Express (75 min, JR Pass covered, departs Shin-Osaka or Tennoji) or taxi (~¥7,000–8,000, 45–60 min). Recommend JR Haruka for cost.
items.address: Kansai International Airport, 1 Senshukukokita, Izumisano, Osaka 549-0001, Japan

items.activity: KIX: check in Asiana, security & duty free
items.notes: Kansai Airport is well laid out. Asiana check-in counters in T1. Buy airport ramen or sushi for an early dinner before departure. · Est. ¥8,000
items.address: Kansai International Airport, 1 Senshukukokita, Izumisano, Osaka 549-0001, Japan

items.notes: Approx 2h flight. Confirmed booking. Arrive Seoul ICN T2.
items.address: Kansai International Airport, 1 Senshukukokita, Izumisano, Osaka 549-0001, Japan

items.notes: Transfer to Grand Hyatt Incheon. Hotel shuttle operates from T2. Short journey — hotel is essentially at the airport.
items.address: Grand Hyatt Incheon, 208 Gonghang-daero, Jung-gu, Incheon 22382, South Korea

items.activity: Grand Hyatt Incheon shuttle & check in
items.notes: Free shuttle from ICN T2. Check in, relax — early start tomorrow. Hotel is connected to the airport complex.
items.address: Grand Hyatt Incheon, 208 Gonghang-daero, Jung-gu, Incheon 22382, South Korea

days.locationAddress: Grand Hyatt Incheon, 208 Gonghang-daero, Jung-gu, Incheon 22382, South Korea

items.notes: No breakfast included at Grand Hyatt. Options: hotel restaurant (pay extra), or head airside early for airport dining. ICN T2 food options open from 06:00.

items.notes: Free hotel shuttle. Confirm pickup time at reception tonight. Journey 10–15 min.
items.address: Incheon International Airport Terminal 2, 272 Gonghang-daero, Jung-gu, Incheon 22382, South Korea

items.notes: Check-in opens 3 hrs before departure. Priority queues for Upper Class (check ticket class). Baggage drop + security should take 30–45 mins. ICN is extremely efficient.

items.notes: Incheon has outstanding duty free — cosmetics, electronics, luxury brands. Good for last-minute Korean purchases if you didnʼt buy in Seoul.
